data.title: Nasdaq reports short interest positions in its stocks as of July 15, 2025: 3,260 securities with 13,792,841,090 shares, down from 14,138,758,851 shares in June.
data.description: In New York on July 25, 2025, it was reported that as of July 15, 2025, there was a short interest in 3,260 Nasdaq Global Market SM securities totaling 13,792,841,090 shares. This shows a decrease from the previous settlement date on June 30, 2025, where there were 14,138,758,851 shares in 3,257 Global Market issues. The short interest in mid-July represented 2.37 days, a decrease from the 2.59 days reported for the prior period.

data.title: NCMEC and Adobe collaborate at Comic-Con to reconstruct the face of San Diego John Doe, inviting public help.
data.description: This week at Comic-Con 2025 in San Diego, the National Center for Missing & Exploited Children (NCMEC) and Adobe have joined forces to showcase one of NCMEC's most perplexing unidentified child cases. Running from July 24 to July 27, this collaboration aims to raise awareness and engage the public in efforts to solve this mystery.

data.title: Carter's (NYSE: CRI) shares drop over tariff concerns, Q2 earnings miss estimate at 17 cents/share on $585.31 million sales (4% YoY). CEO cites pricing investments as cause.
data.description: Shares of Carter's, Inc. (NYSE: CRI) fell on Friday following disappointing earnings results and concerns over increasing tariffs. The company reported adjusted second-quarter earnings per share of 17 cents, falling short of the expected 34 cents. Despite this, quarterly sales reached $585.31 million, a 4% increase compared to the previous year and surpassing the Street's estimate of $561.14 million. Adjusted operating income for the quarter dropped to $11.8 million, down from $39.5 million in the same period last year. Carter's CEO & president, Douglas C. Palladini, expressed disappointment over the decrease in profitability, citing investments in pricing, new stores, and performance-based adjustments as contributing factors.

data.title: IMAX beats Q2 earnings with $0.26 per share, misses sales with $91.68 million. Analysts raise forecasts.
data.description: IMAX Corporation (NYSE: IMAX) released its second-quarter earnings results, showing a mix of strong and weak performance. The company exceeded analyst expectations for earnings, reporting 26 cents per share compared to the estimated 21 cents per share. However, IMAX fell short on quarterly sales, reporting $91.68 million versus the anticipated $92.51 million. CEO Rich Gelfond commented on the results, highlighting the company's success in various aspects such as network expansion globally, record-breaking box office numbers in North America, and gaining market share due to an increase in films utilizing IMAX technology. IMAX's financial performance in the second quarter indicated a positive momentum in key areas of its business.

data.title: OneFootball launches community sale for OneFootball Credits ($OFC) on CoinList, partnering with major brands.
data.description: Berlin, 25th July 2025 - OneFootball is excited to disclose the launch of the official community sale for OneFootball Credits ($OFC). Following the significant initial triumph of OneFootball Club, which introduced key collaborations with FIFA Rivals, Base (by Coinbase), Animoca Brands, Privy, and more, the sale of OFC tokens is now underway.

data.title: Public companies are diversifying their treasuries with ETH, XRP, SOL, and BNB, with 205,000 ETH valued at $1.35B.
data.description: Publicly traded companies are embracing cryptocurrencies beyond Bitcoin, shifting towards Ethereum, Solana, XRP, Binance Coin, and other tokens. SharpLink Gaming, listed on Nasdaq, holds 205,000 ETH worth $1.35 billion, becoming the second-largest ETH holder. Led by Joe Lubin, plans are underway to raise $425 million for its ETH treasury. The company's stock rose from under $4 to nearly $40 post-announcement but settled at $16.29 in July. DeFi Development Corporation, rebranded from Janover, secured $24 million to acquire 395,000 SOL tokens after Kraken executives' involvement. This reflects a broader trend among firms diversifying their financial assets with significant investments in digital currencies.

data.title: Kinetic donates back-to-school supplies to Fort Bend Rainbow Room, supporting local youth.
data.description: In Sugar Land, Texas, local fiber internet provider Kinetic has shown strong community support by donating essential back-to-school supplies to the Fort Bend Rainbow Room. These supplies will assist in supporting emergency and transitional services for children, families, and adults in collaboration with the Texas Department of Family and Protective Services. The nonprofit Fort Bend Rainbow Room will benefit from this donation to aid individuals in need.

data.title: Blackstone (BX) exceeds Q2 expectations with distributable earnings up 25% to $1.57B, assets under management hit $1.2T.
data.description: Blackstone Inc. (NYSE: BX) exceeded expectations in Q2 with a 25% increase in distributable earnings, pushing their total assets under management to a new industry record of over $1.2 trillion. The firm reported Q2 revenues of $3.075 billion, a 22% increase year-over-year, surpassing the analyst estimate of $2.778 billion. Distributable earnings climbed 25% year-over-year to $1.57 billion. Chairman and CEO Stephen A. Schwarzman credited the strong results to growth in key business segments including private wealth, credit, insurance, and infrastructure.

data.title: Electric vehicle market projected to reach $1 trillion by 2029, with a CAGR of 11.5% from 2025-2029. Global market expected to reach $650.32 billion in 2025.
data.description: A report titled "Electric Vehicle and Charging Infrastructure Market: Investment Opportunities and Future Outlook Databook - Q2 2025 Update" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com. The global market for electric vehicles and charging infrastructure is projected to experience a 14.6% annual growth, reaching a value of US$650.32 billion by 2025. The report covers over 50 key performance indicators including market size by value and volume, vehicle type, price point, propulsion type, and location.

data.title: Charter stock falls despite revenue beat; Q2 2025: Revenue $13.77B (0.6% growth), EPS $9.18 - 117,000 internet customers lost.
data.description: Charter Communications (NASDAQ: CHTR) saw a decrease in its stock value following the release of its fiscal Q2 2025 results. The company revealed a 0.6% rise in quarterly revenue to $13.77 billion, surpassing the analyst consensus of $13.76 billion. The earnings per share stood at $9.18, falling short of the anticipated $9.80. Charter's revenue breakdown showed a 24.9% growth in residential mobile service, 2.8% in residential Internet, and 18.9% in other revenue. The company lost 117,000 internet customers compared to a 149,000 decline in Q2 of 2024, which included the impact of about 50,000 disconnects related to a previous event. Charter's potential deal with Cox poses a challenge to Comcast and could exceed AT&T in broadband services. More information is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Coursera's Q2 earnings exceeded expectations: EPS of 12 cents (vs. 9 cents expected), revenue of $187.1 million (vs. $180.48 million). CEO anticipates revenue of $738 to $746 million by 2025. Gained 7 million new learners.
data.description: Coursera, Inc. (NYSE: COUR) surpassed expectations with its second-quarter results announced after Thursday's market close. The company reported earnings of 12 cents per share, surpassing the analyst consensus of nine cents. Quarterly revenue totaled $187.1 million, exceeding the Street's estimate of $180.48 million. CEO Greg Hart highlighted the expanding market opportunity as global demand for acquiring new technology and skills rises. Coursera attracted over seven million new learners seeking to enhance their careers with emerging skills. The company raised its full-year 2025 revenue outlook by $17 million to a range of $738 million to $746 million. Coursera's shares rose by 1.3% at market close.

data.title: Global data center infrastructure market to reach $10.20 billion by 2031 with 16.1% CAGR, driven by data growth and IoT adoption.
data.description: On July 25, 2025, a recent extensive report from The Insight Partners revealed that the worldwide data center infrastructure market is experiencing notable advancement. This growth is attributed to the sharp rise in data generation and the increasing embrace of the Internet of Things (IoT). The market is expected to witness a substantial increase in revenue, with key factors such as rising demand for cloud services and digital transformations contributing to its positive trajectory. The report highlights the fast-paced developments in the industry, signaling promising opportunities for stakeholders.
